channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Surrey Police Investigate City Hall Fire Origin
item.description: Surrey police are investigating a fire that broke out at city hall, with initial indications suggesting the blaze may have originated in a portable toilet. The incident, which occurred on July 14, 202...

item.title: Ad Hoc Committee Exonerates Mchunu of Criminal Charges Amid Governance Concerns
item.description: Parliament's ad hoc committee investigating allegations against police minister Senzo Mchunu has concluded there is no evidence of criminality, though unresolved governance and integrity issues remain...

item.title: Kenya prolongs fuel tax relief to cushion consumers from rising prices
item.description: The Kenyan government has decided to extend the reduced Value Added Tax (VAT) on petroleum products for another three months, keeping the rate at 8% until mid-October. This move aims to protect househ...

item.title: Prosecution Drops Zuleika Lopez as Witness in Sara Duterte Impeachment Trial
item.description: The prosecution has decided to drop Zuleika Lopez, the chief of staff of Vice President Sara Duterte, as a witness in her impeachment trial. This move comes after the National Bureau of Investigation ...

item.title: Senate Hearing Examines Pentagon Comptroller's Role in Oversight of Defense Contracts Linked to Trump's Sons
item.description: Acting Pentagon Comptroller Jules W. Hurst III faced pointed questioning during his Senate Armed Services Committee confirmation hearing regarding billions of dollars in federal defense contracts repo...

item.title: Aged care residents face billing for unused services amid regulatory scrutiny
item.description: Australia's aged care regulator is investigating numerous complaints alleging that aged care providers are charging residents for services they cannot use or do not understand. A class action has also...

item.title: Death of NURTW Chieftain Sparks Panic in Mushin
item.description: Residents in Mushin, Yaba, and Fadeyi areas of Lagos are in chaos after reports of a shooting and the alleged death of Toba Ijaiya, a senior leader of the National Union of Road Transport Workers (NUR...

item.title: AFN national chief urges Ottawa to protect First Nations' rights in major project approval reforms
item.description: Assembly of First Nations (AFN) National Chief Cindy Woodhouse Nepinak used her opening remarks at the AFN general assembly in Ottawa to criticize the federal government's proposed reforms aimed at ac...

item.title: Ann Widdecombe murder: Police investigate targeted attack and motive
item.description: Ann Widdecombe, 78, was found dead at her home in Devon after sustaining serious injuries in an attack believed to have occurred the previous day. Police have confirmed the death was a 'targeted attac...

item.title: New York Imposes Data Center Moratorium, Becomes First State to Do So
item.description: New York has become the first state in the United States to impose a moratorium on the construction of new data centers, citing environmental concerns and the need to manage energy consumption. The de...

item.title: Trump scraps Hormuz fee plan, pivots to Gulf investment deals amid Iran tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump has abandoned a proposal to impose a 20% fee on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, instead opting to pursue trade and investment agreements with Gulf states. The dec...

item.title: South MCA Aspirant Rashid Biliki Arrested for Releasing Raw Sewage on Public Road
item.description: Nairobi Customer Service Chief Officer Geoffrey Mosiria led the operation that resulted in the arrest of South MCA aspirant Rashid Biliki after he was caught discharging raw sewage onto a public road....

item.title: Spain Advances to Final After Dominant Semi-Final Victory Over France
item.description: In a decisive FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final clash in Dallas, Spain comfortably defeated France 2-0, securing their place in the final. The match, marked by Spain's tactical superiority and composure,...

item.title: Pogacar wins Tour de France stage with solo effort on Bastille Day
item.description: Tadej Pogacar secured a solo victory in the 10th stage of the Tour de France on Bastille Day, extending his overall lead over Jonas Vingegaard. The 27-year-old UAE Team Emirates-XRG rider launched a d...

item.title: Beabadoobee Unveils New Track 'Switchblade' from Upcoming Album 'Pylon'
item.description: Beabadoobee has released her new track 'Switchblade', serving as a preview for her fourth studio album 'Pylon', set for release on September 18. The song explores themes of self-defence and uncertaint...

item.title: Trump Announces Withdrawal from Iran Nuclear Deal and Threatens Military Action
item.description: The article details U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement that the 2015 nuclear deal with Iran is effectively over, signaling potential military action against the country. Trump claimed the agre...

item.title: AGRA Recognizes Uyui Dairy Farm's Modern Farming Practices in Tanzania
item.description: A high-level delegation from the Alliance for a Green Revolution in Africa (AGRA) visited Bashe Dairy Farm Limited Tanzania in Uyui District, Tabora Region, to assess its modern milk production and pr...

item.title: Lagos arrests 56-year-old man for illegal waste dumping in drainage
item.description: The Lagos State Government has arrested 56-year-old John Ehidreme for illegally dumping refuse into a drainage channel in Ikorodu. The Commissioner for the Environment and Water Resources, Tokunbo Wah...

item.title: Rajasthan High Court upholds demolition process for unauthorised religious structures near the India-Pakistan border
item.description: The Rajasthan High Court has dismissed petitions challenging the proposed demolition of several mosques, dargahs and madrasas situated within 50 kilometres of the India-Pakistan border. The court obse...

item.title: Mob Justice Incident in Gqeberha Leaves Two Dead and One Critically Injured
item.description: A mob justice incident in Soweto-on-Sea on Tuesday morning resulted in the deaths of two men and left a third critically injured. Police spokesperson Capt Sandra Janse van Rensburg confirmed that offi...
